content:
# Exodus Chapter 26 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~47r–48r (PDF pp. 93–95); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 68–69 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_026_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_026_page_002.jpg`, `EXO_026_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_026_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_026_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE MISHKAN INSTRUCTIONS:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 26 in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter is the architectural core of the tabernacle instructions, moving from the innermost textile layers outward: the linen curtains of the mishkan proper (vv. 1–6), the goat-hair tent covering (vv. 7–14), the acacia-wood frame structure (vv. 15–30), the inner veil separating Holy Place from Holy of Holies (vv. 31–35), and the entrance screen (vv. 36–37). The Hebrew term mishkan ("dwelling-place") dominates — it is the structure that houses the divine Presence. The chapter's concentric logic mirrors the theology: holiness radiates outward from the ark, and the architecture creates graduated zones of access. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 68–69) presents the LXX in continuous uncial script; the LXX versification aligns with the MT throughout, though the LXX characteristically renders shittim ("acacia") as ashepta ("incorruptible") and adds explanatory terms for the textile terminology.

---

## The Inner Curtains of the Tabernacle (vv. 1–6)

**1** And the tabernacle [ve-et-ha-mishkan] you shall make [ta'aseh] with ten [eser] curtains [yeri'ot] of fine twisted linen [shesh moshzar] and blue [u-tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani]; cherubim [keruvim], the work [ma'aseh] of a skilled craftsman [khoshev], you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־הַמִּשְׁכָּן תַּעֲשֶׂה עֶשֶׂר יְרִיעֹת שֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר וּתְכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתֹלַעַת שָׁנִי כְּרֻבִים מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם. **Key terms:** יְרִיעֹת (yeri'ot) = "curtains, tent-panels" — from ירע (yara, "to hang, spread"); the innermost layer of the tabernacle, visible from inside. שֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר (shesh moshzar) = "fine linen, twisted" — shesh is the finest Egyptian linen; moshzar (hophal participle of שׁזר, shazar, "to twist") indicates the threads are plied/twisted for strength. מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב (ma'aseh khoshev) = "work of a skilled craftsman" — literally "work of a thinker/designer"; khoshev (qal participle of חשׁב, khashav, "to think, devise") denotes the highest grade of textile work, where figures are woven into the fabric (not merely embroidered on). The cherubim are integral to the weave itself. LXX: καὶ τὴν σκηνὴν ποιήσεις δέκα αὐλαίας ἐκ βύσσου κεκλωσμένης καὶ ὑακίνθου καὶ πορφύρας καὶ κοκκίνου κεκλωσμένου χερουβιμ ἐργασίᾳ ὑφάντου — αὐλαία ("curtain") for yeri'ah; ὑφάντης ("weaver") for khoshev.

**2** The length [orekh] of one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain [shall be] twenty-eight [shemoneh ve-esrim] cubits [ba-ammah], and the width [ve-rokhav] [shall be] four [arba] cubits [ba-ammah] of the one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain; one [middah] measure [akhat] for all [le-khol] the curtains [ha-yeri'ot].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֹרֶךְ הַיְרִיעָה הָאַחַת שְׁמֹנֶה וְעֶשְׂרִים בָּאַמָּה וְרֹחַב אַרְבַּע בָּאַמָּה הַיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת מִדָּה אַחַת לְכָל־הַיְרִיעוֹת. **Key terms:** The dimensions are 28 cubits long by 4 cubits wide (approximately 42 x 6 feet) per curtain. Ten curtains at 4 cubits each produce a total width of 40 cubits. מִדָּה אַחַת (middah akhat) = "one measure" — all curtains are identical in size. LXX: μήκους τῆς αὐλαίας τῆς μιᾶς ὀκτὼ καὶ εἴκοσι πηχέων — πῆχυς ("cubit") for ammah.

**3** Five [khamesh] of the curtains [ha-yeri'ot] shall be [tihyeyna] joined [khovrot] each [ishshah] to [el] her sister [akhotah], and five [ve-khamesh] curtains [yeri'ot] joined [khovrot] each [ishshah] to [el] her sister [akhotah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads חֲמֵשׁ הַיְרִיעֹת תִּהְיֶיןָ חֹבְרֹת אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ וְחָמֵשׁ יְרִיעֹת חֹבְרֹת אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ. **Key terms:** חֹבְרֹת (khovrot) = qal participle feminine plural of חבר (khavar, "to join, bind together") — "joined, coupled." אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ (ishshah el-akhotah) = "a woman to her sister" — the feminine counterpart of ish el-akhiv ("each to his brother," 25:20); used for the curtain-to-curtain coupling. The ten curtains form two sets of five, which are then joined to make the whole covering. LXX: πέντε αὐλαῖαι ἔσονται συνεχόμεναι ἑτέρα τῇ ἑτέρᾳ — συνέχω ("hold together") for khovrot.

**4** And you shall make [ve-asita] loops [lul'ot] of blue [tekhelet] upon [al] the edge [sefat] of the one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain at the end [mi-qatseh] of the coupling [ba-khovaret], and so [ve-khen] you shall make [ta'aseh] on the edge [bi-sfat] of the curtain [ha-yeri'ah] that is outermost [ha-qitsonah] in the second [ba-makhberet ha-shenit] coupling.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ לֻלְאֹת תְּכֵלֶת עַל שְׂפַת הַיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת מִקָּצָה בַּחֹבָרֶת וְכֵן תַּעֲשֶׂה בִּשְׂפַת הַיְרִיעָה הַקִּיצוֹנָה בַּמַּחְבֶּרֶת הַשֵּׁנִית. **Key terms:** לֻלְאֹת (lul'ot) = "loops" — from a root related to לול (lul, "to twist, spiral"). שְׂפַת (sefat) = "edge, lip" — from שׂפה (safah, "lip, edge, border"). חֹבָרֶת (khovaret) = "coupling, place of joining" — from חבר (khavar, "to join"). קִּיצוֹנָה (qitsonah) = "outermost, extreme" — from קצה (qatseh, "end"); the curtain at the outer edge of the second set. מַּחְבֶּרֶת (makhberet) = "coupling, joining-set" — the assembled group of five curtains. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις αὐταῖς ἀγκύλας ὑακινθίνας ἐπὶ τοῦ χείλους — ἀγκύλη ("loop, clasp") for lul'ah.

**5** Fifty [khamishim] loops [lul'ot] you shall make [ta'aseh] on the one [ba-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain, and fifty [va-khamishim] loops [lul'ot] you shall make [ta'aseh] on the end [bi-qetseh] of the curtain [ha-yeri'ah] that [asher] is in the second [ba-makhberet ha-shenit] coupling; the loops [ha-lul'ot] receiving [maqbilot] each [ishshah] to [el] her sister [akhotah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads חֲמִשִּׁים לֻלָאֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה בַּיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת וַחֲמִשִּׁים לֻלָאֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה בִּקְצֵה הַיְרִיעָה אֲשֶׁר בַּמַּחְבֶּרֶת הַשֵּׁנִית מַקְבִּילֹת הַלֻּלָאֹת אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ. **Key terms:** מַקְבִּילֹת (maqbilot) = hiphil participle feminine plural of קבל (qaval, "to receive, correspond") — "receiving, corresponding to, facing"; the loops on each set must align exactly so the clasps can unite them. LXX: ἀντιπίπτουσαι ἀντιπρόσωποι ἑκάστη — ἀντιπίπτω ("fall opposite, correspond") for maqbilot.

**6** And you shall make [ve-asita] fifty [khamishim] clasps [qarsei] of gold [zahav], and you shall join [ve-khibbarta] the curtains [et-ha-yeri'ot] each [ishshah] to [el] her sister [akhotah] with the clasps [ba-qerasim], and the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] shall be [ve-hayah] one [ekhad].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ חֲמִשִּׁים קַרְסֵי זָהָב וְחִבַּרְתָּ אֶת־הַיְרִיעֹת אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ בַּקְּרָסִים וְהָיָה הַמִּשְׁכָּן אֶחָד. **Key terms:** קַרְסֵי (qarsei) = "clasps, hooks" — from קרס (qaras, "to hook, clasp"); gold hooks that pass through the matching loops. וְחִבַּרְתָּ (ve-khibbarta) = piel perfect of חבר (khavar, "to join") — "and you shall join/couple." וְהָיָה הַמִּשְׁכָּן אֶחָד (ve-hayah ha-mishkan ekhad) = "and the tabernacle shall be one" — the theological point: the two halves, once clasped, become a unified whole. The gold clasps binding the innermost curtains contrast with the bronze clasps of v. 11 for the outer tent — materials correspond to proximity to the divine Presence. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις κρίκους πεντήκοντα χρυσοῦς καὶ συνάψεις τὰς αὐλαίας... καὶ ἔσται ἡ σκηνὴ μία — κρίκος ("ring, clasp") for qeres.

---

## The Goat-Hair Tent Covering (vv. 7–14)

**7** And you shall make [ve-asita] curtains [yeri'ot] of goat hair [izzim] for a tent [le-ohel] over [al] the tabernacle [ha-mishkan]; eleven [ashtei-esreh] curtains [yeri'ot] you shall make [ta'aseh] them [otam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ יְרִיעֹת עִזִּים לְאֹהֶל עַל־הַמִּשְׁכָּן עַשְׁתֵּי־עֶשְׂרֵה יְרִיעֹת תַּעֲשֶׂה אֹתָם. **Key terms:** עִזִּים (izzim) = "goats" — i.e., goat hair; the outer tent-layer over the linen curtains. לְאֹהֶל (le-ohel) = "for a tent" — distinguished from the mishkan beneath; ohel is the tent proper (the covering), while mishkan is the dwelling (the inner structure). עַשְׁתֵּי־עֶשְׂרֵה (ashtei-esreh) = "eleven" — one more curtain than the inner set, providing extra coverage. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις δέρρεις τριχίνας σκέπην ἐπὶ τῆς σκηνῆς — δέρρις ("skin covering, curtain") for yeri'ah; τρίχινος ("of hair") for izzim.

**8** The length [orekh] of one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain [shall be] thirty [sheloshim] cubits [ba-ammah], and the width [ve-rokhav] [shall be] four [arba] cubits [ba-ammah] of the one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain; one [middah] measure [akhat] for the eleven [le-ashtei esreh] curtains [yeri'ot].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֹרֶךְ הַיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת שְׁלֹשִׁים בָּאַמָּה וְרֹחַב אַרְבַּע בָּאַמָּה הַיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת מִדָּה אַחַת לְעַשְׁתֵּי עֶשְׂרֵה יְרִיעֹת. The goat-hair curtains are 30 cubits long (two cubits longer than the inner linen curtains), providing overhang.

**9** And you shall join [ve-khibbarta] five [et-khamesh] of the curtains [ha-yeri'ot] by themselves [le-vad] and six [ve-et-shesh] of the curtains [ha-yeri'ot] by themselves [le-vad], and you shall fold over [ve-khafalta] the sixth [et-ha-yeri'ah ha-shishit] curtain toward the front [el-mul penei] of the tent [ha-ohel].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְחִבַּרְתָּ אֶת־חֲמֵשׁ הַיְרִיעֹת לְבָד וְאֶת־שֵׁשׁ הַיְרִיעֹת לְבָד וְכָפַלְתָּ אֶת־הַיְרִיעָה הַשִּׁשִׁית אֶל־מוּל פְּנֵי הָאֹהֶל. **Key terms:** לְבָד (le-vad) = "by itself, separately" — the goat-hair curtains are divided into sets of five and six (not equal halves). וְכָפַלְתָּ (ve-khafalta) = qal perfect of כפל (kafal, "to fold, double over") — the sixth curtain of the front set is doubled over at the entrance, creating a decorative drape or reinforced front. LXX: καὶ συνάψεις τὰς πέντε δέρρεις ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτὸ καὶ τὰς ἓξ δέρρεις ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό — ἐπὶ τὸ αὐτό ("together") for le-vad.

**10** And you shall make [ve-asita] fifty [khamishim] loops [lul'ot] upon [al] the edge [sefat] of the one [ha-yeri'ah ha-akhat] curtain, the outermost [ha-qitsonah] in the coupling [ba-khoveret], and fifty [va-khamishim] loops [lul'ot] upon [al] the edge [sefat] of the curtain [ha-yeri'ah] of the second [ha-shenit] coupling [ha-makhberet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ חֲמִשִּׁים לֻלָאֹת עַל שְׂפַת הַיְרִיעָה הָאֶחָת הַקִּיצֹנָה בַּחֹבָרֶת וַחֲמִשִּׁים לֻלָאֹת עַל שְׂפַת הַיְרִיעָה הַשֵּׁנִית הַמַּחְבָּרֶת. Same loop-and-clasp joining system as the inner curtains (vv. 4–5), but here for the goat-hair layer.

**11** And you shall make [ve-asita] fifty [khamishim] clasps [qarsei] of bronze [nekhoshet], and you shall bring [ve-heveta] the clasps [et-ha-qerasim] into the loops [ba-lul'ot], and you shall join [ve-khibbarta] the tent [et-ha-ohel] and it shall be [ve-hayah] one [ekhad].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ קַרְסֵי נְחֹשֶׁת חֲמִשִּׁים וְהֵבֵאתָ אֶת־הַקְּרָסִים בַּלֻּלָאֹת וְחִבַּרְתָּ אֶת־הָאֹהֶל וְהָיָה אֶחָד. **Key terms:** קַרְסֵי נְחֹשֶׁת (qarsei nekhoshet) = "clasps of bronze" — the outer tent uses bronze clasps, not gold (cf. v. 6). The material hierarchy (gold for the mishkan curtains, bronze for the ohel tent) reflects the graduated holiness of the layers. וְהָיָה אֶחָד (ve-hayah ekhad) = "and it shall be one" — paralleling v. 6; the tent, like the tabernacle, achieves unity through joining. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις κρίκους χαλκοῦς πεντήκοντα — χαλκοῦς ("of bronze") for nekhoshet.

**12** And the overhanging part [ve-serekh], the excess [ha-odef] of the curtains [bi-yeri'ot] of the tent [ha-ohel] — the half [khatsi] of the curtain [ha-yeri'ah] that is left over [ha-odefet] shall hang over [tisrakh] the back [al akhorei] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְסֶרַח הָעֹדֵף בִּירִיעֹת הָאֹהֶל חֲצִי הַיְרִיעָה הָעֹדֶפֶת תִּסְרַח עַל אֲחֹרֵי הַמִּשְׁכָּן. **Key terms:** סֶרַח (serekh) = "overhang, excess, that which hangs over" — from סרח (sarakh, "to hang down, overhang"). הָעֹדֵף (ha-odef) = "the excess, the surplus" — qal participle of עדף (adaf, "to be in excess"). The goat-hair curtains, being wider in total than the linen curtains, create an overhang at the rear of the structure. LXX: καὶ ὑποθήσεις τὸ πλεονάζον ἐν ταῖς δέρρεσιν τῆς σκηνῆς — πλεονάζω ("be in excess") for odef.

**13** And the cubit [ve-ha-ammah] from this side [mi-zeh] and the cubit [ve-ha-ammah] from that side [mi-zeh] of the excess [ba-odef] in the length [be-orekh] of the curtains [yeri'ot] of the tent [ha-ohel] shall be [yihyeh] hanging over [sarukh] upon [al] the sides [tsiddei] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] from this side [mi-zeh] and from that side [u-mi-zeh] to cover it [le-khassoto].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָאַמָּה מִזֶּה וְהָאַמָּה מִזֶּה בָּעֹדֵף בְּאֹרֶךְ יְרִיעֹת הָאֹהֶל יִהְיֶה סָרוּחַ עַל־צִדֵּי הַמִּשְׁכָּן מִזֶּה וּמִזֶּה לְכַסֹּתוֹ. **Key terms:** סָרוּחַ (sarukh) = qal passive participle of סרח (sarakh, "to hang over") — "hanging over." לְכַסֹּתוֹ (le-khassoto) = piel infinitive construct of כסה (kasah, "to cover") + 3ms suffix — "to cover it." The extra two cubits of length (30 vs. 28) allow one cubit of overhang on each side, providing additional protection. LXX: πῆχυν ἐκ τούτου καὶ πῆχυν ἐκ τούτου ἐκ τοῦ ὑπερέχοντος — ὑπερέχω ("exceed, project") for odef.

**14** And you shall make [ve-asita] a covering [mikhseh] for the tent [la-ohel] of skins of rams [orot eilim] made red [me'oddamim], and a covering [u-mikhseh] of skins of takhash [orot tekhashim] from above [mi-lema'lah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ מִכְסֶה לָאֹהֶל עֹרֹת אֵילִם מְאָדָּמִים וּמִכְסֶה עֹרֹת תְּחָשִׁים מִלְמָעְלָה. **Key terms:** מִכְסֶה (mikhseh) = "covering" — from כסה (kasah, "to cover"); a protective outer layer. The structure now has four layers from inside out: (1) linen curtains with cherubim, (2) goat-hair tent curtains, (3) red-dyed ram skins, (4) takhash skins on top. מִלְמָעְלָה (mi-lema'lah) = "from above, on top" — the takhash skins form the outermost, weather-facing layer. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις κατακάλυμμα τῇ σκηνῇ δέρματα κριῶν ἠρυθροδανωμένα καὶ ἐπικαλύμματα δέρματα ὑακίνθινα ἐπάνωθεν — κατακάλυμμα ("covering") for mikhseh; ὑακίνθινα ("hyacinth-colored") for tekhashim.

---

## The Acacia Frames of the Tabernacle (vv. 15–30)

**15** And you shall make [ve-asita] the frames [et-ha-qerashim] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood, standing upright [omedim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־הַקְּרָשִׁים לַמִּשְׁכָּן עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים עֹמְדִים. **Key terms:** קְרָשִׁים (qerashim) = "frames, boards, planks" — from קרשׁ (qarash); traditionally rendered "boards," but archaeological and linguistic evidence suggests open frames or lattice-like panels rather than solid planks. עֹמְדִים (omedim) = qal participle of עמד (amad, "to stand") — "standing, upright"; the frames stand vertically. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις στύλους τῇ σκηνῇ ἐκ ξύλων ἀσήπτων ἑστῶτας — στῦλος ("pillar, post") for qeresh; ἑστώς ("standing") for omedim.

**16** Ten [eser] cubits [ammot] the length [orekh] of the frame [ha-qaresh], and a cubit [ammah] and a half [va-khetsi] cubit [ha-ammah] the width [rokhav] of the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads עֶשֶׂר אַמּוֹת אֹרֶךְ הַקָּרֶשׁ וְאַמָּה וַחֲצִי הָאַמָּה רֹחַב הַקָּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד. Each frame is 10 cubits tall (approximately 15 feet) and 1.5 cubits wide (approximately 2.25 feet). LXX: δέκα πήχεων τὸν στῦλον τὸν ἕνα — standard equivalent.

**17** Two [shtei] tenons [yadot] for the one [la-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame, joined [meshullavot] each [ishshah] to [el] her sister [akhotah]; thus [ken] you shall make [ta'aseh] for all [le-khol] the frames [qarshei] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שְׁתֵּי יָדוֹת לַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד מְשֻׁלָּבוֹת אִשָּׁה אֶל־אֲחֹתָהּ כֵּן תַּעֲשֶׂה לְכֹל קַרְשֵׁי הַמִּשְׁכָּן. **Key terms:** יָדוֹת (yadot) = "hands, tenons, projections" — from יד (yad, "hand"); the projecting tenon-pegs at the base of each frame that fit into the silver sockets. מְשֻׁלָּבוֹת (meshullavot) = pual participle of שׁלב (shalav, "to join, set in order") — "joined, set together"; the two tenons are parallel and aligned. LXX: δύο ἀγκωνίσκους τῷ στύλῳ τῷ ἑνὶ ἀντιπίπτοντας ἕτερον τῷ ἑτέρῳ — ἀγκωνίσκος ("elbow, projection") for yad.

**18** And you shall make [ve-asita] the frames [et-ha-qerashim] for the tabernacle [la-mishkan]: twenty [esrim] frames [qerashim] for the south [negev] side [li-fe'at], southward [teimanah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ אֶת־הַקְּרָשִׁים לַמִּשְׁכָּן עֶשְׂרִים קֶרֶשׁ לִפְאַת נֶגְבָּה תֵימָנָה. **Key terms:** נֶגְבָּה תֵימָנָה (negbah teimanah) = "southward, toward the south" — a double directional indicator: negev = "south" (lit. "dry land"), teimanah = "to the right hand" (south when facing east). Twenty frames at 1.5 cubits each = 30 cubits total wall length. LXX: εἴκοσι στύλους ἐκ τοῦ κλίτους τοῦ πρὸς νότον — νότος ("south") for negev.

**19** And forty [ve-arba'im] sockets [adnei] of silver [khesef] you shall make [ta'aseh] under [takhat] the twenty [esrim] frames [ha-qerashim]; two [shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame for its two [li-shtei] tenons [yadotav], and two [u-shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame for its two [li-shtei] tenons [yadotav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַרְבָּעִים אַדְנֵי־כֶסֶף תַּעֲשֶׂה תַּחַת עֶשְׂרִים הַקָּרֶשׁ שְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד לִשְׁתֵּי יָדֹתָיו וּשְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד לִשְׁתֵּי יָדֹתָיו. **Key terms:** אֲדָנִים (adanim) = "sockets, bases, pedestals" — from אדן (eden, "base, pedestal"); silver foundation blocks into which the wooden tenons are inserted. Each socket weighs one talent of silver (38:27). The forty sockets (two per frame) form the silver foundation of the south wall. LXX: καὶ τεσσαράκοντα βάσεις ἀργυρᾶς ποιήσεις — βάσις ("base") for eden.

**20** And for the second [ha-shenit] side [u-le-tsela] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], for the north [ha-tsafon] side [le-fe'at], twenty [esrim] frames [qeresh],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּלְצֶלַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן הַשֵּׁנִית לִפְאַת צָפוֹן עֶשְׂרִים קָרֶשׁ. **Key terms:** צֶלַע (tsela) = "side, rib" — the same word used for Adam's "rib" (Gen 2:21–22); here the structural side-wall. צָפוֹן (tsafon) = "north" — from צפן (tsafan, "to hide, treasure up"). LXX: καὶ τὸ κλίτος τὸ δεύτερον τὸ πρὸς βορρᾶν — βορρᾶς ("north wind, north") for tsafon.

**21** and their forty [ve-arba'im] sockets [adneihem] of silver [khesef]: two [shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame and two [u-shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַרְבָּעִים אַדְנֵיהֶם כָּסֶף שְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד וּשְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד. The north wall mirrors the south wall exactly: twenty frames, forty silver sockets.

**22** And for the rear [u-le-yarketei] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], westward [yammah], you shall make [ta'aseh] six [shishah] frames [qerashim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּלְיַרְכְּתֵי הַמִּשְׁכָּן יָמָּה תַּעֲשֶׂה שִׁשָּׁה קְרָשִׁים. **Key terms:** יַרְכְּתֵי (yarketei) = "rear parts, hindquarters" — from ירך (yarekh, "thigh, rear"); the back or western end of the tabernacle. יָמָּה (yammah) = "seaward, westward" — from ים (yam, "sea"); the Mediterranean lay to the west of Israel, so "seaward" = "westward." Six frames at 1.5 cubits = 9 cubits width for the rear wall. LXX: καὶ ἐκ τῶν ὀπίσω τῆς σκηνῆς κατὰ τὸ μέρος τὸ πρὸς θάλασσαν — θάλασσα ("sea") for yammah.

**23** And two [u-shnei] frames [qerashim] you shall make [ta'aseh] for the corners [le-mequtse'ot] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] at the rear [ba-yarketayim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּשְׁנֵי קְרָשִׁים תַּעֲשֶׂה לִמְקֻצְעֹת הַמִּשְׁכָּן בַּיַּרְכָתָיִם. **Key terms:** מְקֻצְעֹת (mequtse'ot) = "corners, corner-pieces" — from קצע (qatsa, "to cut off, scrape"); specially shaped corner-frames where the rear wall meets the side walls. בַּיַּרְכָתָיִם (ba-yarketayim) = "at the two rear sections" — dual form of yarekh. LXX: καὶ δύο στύλους ποιήσεις ἐπὶ τῶν γωνιῶν — γωνία ("corner, angle") for mequtse'ot.

**24** And they shall be [ve-yihyu] paired [to'amim] from below [mi-lemattah], and together [yakhdav] they shall be [yihyu] complete [tammim] upon [al] its top [rosho], unto [el] the one [ha-tabb'at ha-akhat] ring; thus [ken] it shall be [yihyeh] for both of them [li-shneihem]; for the two [li-shnei] corners [ha-miqtso'ot] they shall be [yihyu].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְיִהְיוּ תֹאֲמִם מִלְּמַטָּה וְיַחְדָּו יִהְיוּ תַמִּים עַל־רֹאשׁוֹ אֶל־הַטַּבַּעַת הָאֶחָת כֵּן יִהְיֶה לִשְׁנֵיהֶם לִשְׁנֵי הַמִּקְצֹעֹת יִהְיוּ. **Key terms:** תֹאֲמִם (to'amim) = "twinned, paired" — from תאם (ta'am, "to be twin"); the corner frames are joined as twins. תַמִּים (tammim) = "complete, whole" — from תמם (tamam, "to be complete"); they form a unified whole at the top. This verse describes a complex joinery where each corner frame is doubled/twinned from bottom to top, fastened together with a single ring at the top. LXX: ἔσονται ἐξ ἴσου κάτωθεν κατὰ τὸ αὐτό — ἐξ ἴσου ("equally") for to'amim.

**25** And there shall be [ve-hayu] eight [shemonah] frames [qerashim] and their sockets [ve-adneihem] of silver [khesef], sixteen [shishah asar] sockets [adanim]; two [shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame and two [u-shnei] sockets [adanim] under [takhat] the one [ha-qaresh ha-ekhad] frame.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיוּ שְׁמֹנָה קְרָשִׁים וְאַדְנֵיהֶם כֶּסֶף שִׁשָּׁה עָשָׂר אֲדָנִים שְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד וּשְׁנֵי אֲדָנִים תַּחַת הַקֶּרֶשׁ הָאֶחָד. The west wall has eight frames total (six regular plus two corner-frames), with sixteen silver sockets. Total sockets: 40 (south) + 40 (north) + 16 (west) = 96 silver sockets for the frame structure.

**26** And you shall make [ve-asita] bars [verikhai] of acacia [atsei shittim] wood: five [khamishah] for the frames [le-qarshei] of the one [ha-ekhad] side [tsela] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ בְרִיחֵי עֲצֵי שִׁטִּים חֲמִשָּׁה לְקַרְשֵׁי צֶלַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן הָאֶחָד. **Key terms:** בְרִיחֵי (verikhai) = "bars of" — from בריח (beriakh, "bar, bolt"); horizontal crossbars that run through the frames to bind them together laterally. Five bars per wall provide structural rigidity. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις μοχλοὺς ἐκ ξύλων ἀσήπτων — μοχλός ("bar, bolt") for beriakh.

**27** and five [va-khamishah] bars [verikkim] for the frames [le-qarshei] of the second [ha-shenit] side [tsela] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan], and five [va-khamishah] bars [verikkim] for the frames [le-qarshei] of the side [tsela] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] at the rear [la-yarketayim], westward [yammah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַחֲמִשָּׁה בְרִיחִם לְקַרְשֵׁי צֶלַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן הַשֵּׁנִית וַחֲמִשָּׁה בְרִיחִם לְקַרְשֵׁי צֶלַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן לַיַּרְכָתַיִם יָמָּה. Fifteen bars total: five for the south wall, five for the north wall, five for the west wall.

**28** And the middle [ha-tikhon] bar [ve-ha-beriakh] in the midst [be-tokh] of the frames [ha-qerashim] shall run through [mavriakh] from end [min-ha-qatseh] to [el] end [ha-qatseh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַבְּרִיחַ הַתִּיכֹן בְּתוֹךְ הַקְּרָשִׁים מַבְרִחַ מִן־הַקָּצֶה אֶל־הַקָּצֶה. **Key terms:** הַתִּיכֹן (ha-tikhon) = "the middle one" — from תוך (tokh, "midst"); the central bar of the five. מַבְרִחַ (mavriakh) = hiphil participle of ברח (barakh, "to flee, pass through") — "running through, passing through"; the middle bar extends the full length of the wall, passing through all the frames. The Talmud (Shabbat 98b) states this bar ran through holes bored in the middle of each frame. LXX: καὶ ὁ μοχλὸς ὁ μέσος ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν στύλων διικνείσθω — διικνέομαι ("reach through") for mavriakh.

**29** And the frames [ve-et-ha-qerashim] you shall overlay [tetsappeh] with gold [zahav], and their rings [ve-et-tabb'oteihem] you shall make [ta'aseh] of gold [zahav], as holders [batim] for the bars [la-berikkim]; and you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] the bars [et-ha-berikkim] with gold [zahav].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאֶת־הַקְּרָשִׁים תְּצַפֶּה זָהָב וְאֶת־טַבְּעֹתֵיהֶם תַּעֲשֶׂה זָהָב בָּתִּים לַבְּרִיחִם וְצִפִּיתָ אֶת־הַבְּרִיחִם זָהָב. **Key terms:** בָּתִּים (batim) = "houses, holders" — from בית (bayit, "house"); the rings serve as housings through which the bars pass (cf. 25:27). The gold-overlaid frames and bars form the glittering interior walls visible through the linen curtain panels. LXX: καὶ τοὺς στύλους καταχρυσώσεις χρυσίῳ — καταχρυσόω ("gild") for tetsappeh.

**30** And you shall set up [va-haqemota] the tabernacle [et-ha-mishkan] according to its plan [ke-mishpato] which [asher] you were shown [horeita] on the mountain [ba-har].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַהֲקֵמֹתָ אֶת־הַמִּשְׁכָּן כְּמִשְׁפָּטוֹ אֲשֶׁר הָרְאֵיתָ בָּהָר. **Key terms:** וַהֲקֵמֹתָ (va-haqemota) = hiphil perfect of קום (qum, "to arise, stand") — "and you shall cause to stand, you shall erect." כְּמִשְׁפָּטוֹ (ke-mishpato) = "according to its plan/ordinance" — from משׁפט (mishpat, "judgment, ordinance, manner, plan"); the tabernacle must be erected according to the divinely shown pattern. This echoes the tavnit language of 25:9, 40. הָרְאֵיתָ (horeita) = hophal perfect of ראה (ra'ah, "to see") — "you were shown, you were caused to see"; Moses passively received the vision. LXX: καὶ ἀναστήσεις τὴν σκηνὴν κατὰ τὸ εἶδος τὸ δεδειγμένον σοι ἐν τῷ ὄρει — εἶδος ("form, appearance") for mishpat; δείκνυμι ("show") for horeita.

---

## The Veil — The Parokhet (vv. 31–35)

**31** And you shall make [ve-asita] a veil [farokhet] of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and fine twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar]; the work [ma'aseh] of a skilled craftsman [khoshev] he shall make [ya'aseh] it [otah] with cherubim [keruvim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ פָרֹכֶת תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר מַעֲשֵׂה חֹשֵׁב יַעֲשֶׂה אֹתָהּ כְּרֻבִים. **Key terms:** פָּרֹכֶת (farokhet/parokhet) = "veil, curtain" — the inner veil that separates the Holy Place from the Holy of Holies; the most theologically significant textile in the tabernacle. The etymology is debated; possibly from פרך (parakh, "to divide, separate") or an Akkadian cognate. This veil is torn at the crucifixion (Matt 27:51). Like the innermost curtains (v. 1), it is woven with cherubim by the ma'aseh khoshev technique — the highest craftsmanship. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις καταπέτασμα ἐξ ὑακίνθου καὶ πορφύρας καὶ κοκκίνου κεκλωσμένου καὶ βύσσου κεκλωσμένης ἔργον ὑφαντόν — καταπέτασμα ("curtain, veil") for parokhet; this is the term used in the NT for the temple veil (Matt 27:51; Heb 6:19, 9:3, 10:20).

**32** And you shall put [ve-natattah] it [otah] upon [al] four [arba'ah] pillars [ammudei] of acacia [shittim] overlaid [metsuppim] with gold [zahav]; their hooks [vaveihem] of gold [zahav], upon [al] four [arba'ah] sockets [adnei] of silver [khasef].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּה אֹתָהּ עַל־אַרְבָּעָה עַמּוּדֵי שִׁטִּים מְצֻפִּים זָהָב וָוֵיהֶם זָהָב עַל־אַרְבָּעָה אַדְנֵי־כָסֶף. **Key terms:** עַמּוּדֵי (ammudei) = "pillars of" — from עמוד (ammud, "pillar, column"). מְצֻפִּים (metsuppim) = pual participle of צפה (tsafah, "to overlay") — "overlaid." וָוֵיהֶם (vaveihem) = "their hooks" — from וָו (vav, "hook"); the letter vav in the Hebrew alphabet is named for this hook shape. The veil hangs from gold hooks on gold-overlaid acacia pillars set in silver sockets (cf. the bronze sockets of the entrance screen, v. 37 — the material hierarchy continues). LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσεις αὐτὸ ἐπὶ τεσσάρων στύλων ἀσήπτων κεχρυσωμένων χρυσίῳ — κεχρυσωμένος ("gilded") for metsuppim.

**33** And you shall hang [ve-natattah] the veil [et-ha-parokhet] under [takhat] the clasps [ha-qerasim], and you shall bring [ve-heveta] there [shammah], within [mi-beit] the veil [la-parokhet], the ark [et aron] of the testimony [ha-edut]; and the veil [ha-parokhet] shall separate [ve-hivdilah] for you [lakhem] between [bein] the Holy Place [ha-qodesh] and [u-vein] the Holy [qodesh] of Holies [ha-qodashim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּה אֶת־הַפָּרֹכֶת תַּחַת הַקְּרָסִים וְהֵבֵאתָ שָׁמָּה מִבֵּית לַפָּרֹכֶת אֵת אֲרוֹן הָעֵדוּת וְהִבְדִּילָה הַפָּרֹכֶת לָכֶם בֵּין הַקֹּדֶשׁ וּבֵין קֹדֶשׁ הַקֳּדָשִׁים. **Key terms:** תַּחַת הַקְּרָסִים (takhat ha-qerasim) = "under the clasps" — the veil is positioned directly beneath the fifty gold clasps that join the two halves of the linen curtains (v. 6); the seam-line above marks the boundary-line below. וְהִבְדִּילָה (ve-hivdilah) = hiphil perfect of בדל (badal, "to separate, divide") — "and it shall separate/divide"; the same verb used in creation (Gen 1:4, 6, 7, 14, 18) to divide light from darkness, waters from waters. The parokhet is a creation-boundary within the sanctuary. קֹדֶשׁ הַקֳּדָשִׁים (qodesh ha-qodashim) = "holy of holies" — the superlative construction; the innermost chamber where the ark resides. LXX: καὶ διοριεῖ τὸ καταπέτασμα ὑμῖν ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ ἁγίου καὶ ἀνὰ μέσον τοῦ ἁγίου τῶν ἁγίων — τὸ ἅγιον τῶν ἁγίων ("the holy of holies") for qodesh ha-qodashim.

**34** And you shall put [ve-natatta] the mercy seat [et-ha-kapporet] upon [al] the ark [aron] of the testimony [ha-edut] in the Holy [be-qodesh] of Holies [ha-qodashim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְנָתַתָּ אֶת־הַכַּפֹּרֶת עַל אֲרוֹן הָעֵדֻת בְּקֹדֶשׁ הַקֳּדָשִׁים. The kapporet is placed on the ark, and the ensemble is placed behind the parokhet, in the innermost chamber. LXX: καὶ θήσεις τὸ ἱλαστήριον ἐπὶ τὴν κιβωτὸν τῶν μαρτυρίων ἐν τῷ ἁγίῳ τῶν ἁγίων — ἱλαστήριον ("mercy seat") for kapporet.

**35** And you shall set [ve-samta] the table [et-ha-shulkhan] outside [mi-khuts] the veil [la-parokhet], and the lampstand [ve-et-ha-menorah] opposite [nokhakh] the table [ha-shulkhan] upon [al] the side [tsela] of the tabernacle [ha-mishkan] southward [teimanah]; and the table [ve-ha-shulkhan] you shall put [titten] upon [al] the north [tsafon] side [tsela].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׂמְתָּ אֶת־הַשֻּׁלְחָן מִחוּץ לַפָּרֹכֶת וְאֶת־הַמְּנֹרָה נֹכַח הַשֻּׁלְחָן עַל צֶלַע הַמִּשְׁכָּן תֵּימָנָה וְהַשֻּׁלְחָן תִּתֵּן עַל צֶלַע צָפוֹן. **Key terms:** מִחוּץ לַפָּרֹכֶת (mi-khuts la-parokhet) = "outside the veil" — in the Holy Place, not the Holy of Holies. נֹכַח (nokhakh) = "opposite, in front of, facing" — the lampstand faces the table across the room. The spatial arrangement: the table of the Bread of the Presence on the north side, the lampstand on the south side, facing each other across the Holy Place. The altar of incense (30:1–6) will later be placed between them, in front of the veil. LXX: καὶ θήσεις τὴν τράπεζαν ἔξωθεν τοῦ καταπετάσματος — ἔξωθεν ("outside") for mi-khuts.

---

## The Screen for the Entrance (vv. 36–37)

**36** And you shall make [ve-asita] a screen [masakh] for the entrance [le-fetakh] of the tent [ha-ohel], of blue [tekhelet] and purple [ve-argaman] and scarlet yarn [ve-tola'at shani] and fine twisted linen [ve-shesh moshzar], the work [ma'aseh] of an embroiderer [roqem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ מָסָךְ לְפֶתַח הָאֹהֶל תְּכֵלֶת וְאַרְגָּמָן וְתוֹלַעַת שָׁנִי וְשֵׁשׁ מָשְׁזָר מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵם. **Key terms:** מָסָךְ (masakh) = "screen, covering" — from סכך (sakhakh, "to cover, screen"); a hanging screen at the entrance, distinct from the parokhet (the inner veil). פֶּתַח (petakh) = "entrance, opening, doorway" — from פתח (patakh, "to open"). מַעֲשֵׂה רֹקֵם (ma'aseh roqem) = "work of an embroiderer" — roqem (qal participle of רקם, raqam, "to variegate, embroider") denotes a lower grade of textile work than khoshev (v. 1, 31); embroidery is applied to the surface, while khoshev weaving is integral to the fabric. Note: the entrance screen has no cherubim specified — only the innermost curtains and the parokhet bear the cherub design. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις ἐπίσπαστρον ἐξ ὑακίνθου... ἔργον ποικιλτοῦ — ἐπίσπαστρον ("screen, draw-curtain") for masakh; ποικιλτής ("embroiderer") for roqem.

**37** And you shall make [ve-asita] for the screen [la-masakh] five [khamishah] pillars [ammudei] of acacia [shittim], and you shall overlay [ve-tsippita] them [otam] with gold [zahav], and their hooks [vaveihem] of gold [zahav]; and you shall cast [ve-yatsaqta] for them [lahem] five [khamishah] sockets [adnei] of bronze [nekhoshet].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעָשִׂיתָ לַמָּסָךְ חֲמִשָּׁה עַמּוּדֵי שִׁטִּים וְצִפִּיתָ אֹתָם זָהָב וָוֵיהֶם זָהָב וְיָצַקְתָּ לָהֶם חֲמִשָּׁה אַדְנֵי נְחֹשֶׁת. **Key terms:** The entrance screen has five pillars (vs. four for the parokhet, v. 32) and, critically, bronze sockets instead of silver. The material gradient from inside out: the parokhet pillars stand in silver sockets; the entrance pillars stand in bronze sockets. Gold hooks are used for both, but the foundation material degrades as one moves away from the Holy of Holies. This architectural hierarchy encodes the theology of graduated holiness — the nearer to YHWH's dwelling, the more precious the materials. LXX: καὶ ποιήσεις τῷ καταπετάσματι πέντε στύλους... καὶ χωνεύσεις αὐτοῖς πέντε βάσεις χαλκᾶς — χωνεύω ("cast, smelt") for ve-yatsaqta.

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 26 is the architectural heart of the tabernacle instructions — the chapter where the dwelling itself takes shape. The text moves concentrically from the innermost textile layer (the linen curtains with cherubim, vv. 1–6) outward through the goat-hair tent (vv. 7–14), then describes the wooden frame skeleton (vv. 15–30), and finally defines the two critical boundary-curtains: the parokhet separating Holy Place from Holy of Holies (vv. 31–35), and the entrance screen (vv. 36–37). The organization is not haphazard; it follows the logic of holiness, beginning at the layer closest to the divine Presence and working outward toward the human approach.

The key verb in vv. 1–6 is khavar ("to join, couple"), repeated in multiple forms. The ten curtains are sewn into two sets of five, then clasped together with fifty gold clasps to form "one" (ekhad) tabernacle. The language is deliberately evocative: "a woman to her sister" (ishshah el-akhotah, v. 3) describes the curtain-joining, mirroring the "each to his brother" (ish el-akhiv) of the cherubim in 25:20. The tabernacle is a fabric of relationships, each element paired and coupled. The resulting unity — "and the tabernacle shall be one" (ve-hayah ha-mishkan ekhad, v. 6) — echoes the theological declaration of Deuteronomy 6:4.

The material hierarchy throughout the chapter encodes graduated holiness with remarkable consistency. The linen curtains (innermost) are joined with gold clasps (v. 6); the goat-hair tent (middle) with bronze clasps (v. 11). The parokhet's pillars stand in silver sockets (v. 32); the entrance screen's pillars in bronze sockets (v. 37). The innermost curtains and the parokhet are woven with cherubim by the ma'aseh khoshev technique (the highest textile craft, vv. 1, 31); the entrance screen is the work of a roqem (embroiderer, v. 36), a lesser craft, and has no cherubim specified. Every material choice maps to proximity to the divine Presence.

The parokhet (v. 33) performs the act of hivdilah — "separating" — using the same verb (badal) that structures the creation narrative (Gen 1:4, 6, 7, 14, 18). The veil is a creation-boundary within the sanctuary, dividing the qodesh from the qodesh ha-qodashim as God divided light from darkness. Its placement "under the clasps" (takhat ha-qerasim, v. 33) means the seam-line of the ceiling curtains directly marks the boundary between the two chambers — the architecture is unified from floor to ceiling.

The instruction to erect the tabernacle "according to its plan which you were shown on the mountain" (ke-mishpato asher horeita ba-har, v. 30) uses mishpat — ordinarily "judgment" or "ordinance" — for the architectural pattern, suggesting that the building plan has the force of legal decree. The heavenly archetype revealed to Moses (cf. 25:9, 40) is not merely aesthetic but juridical: to build incorrectly would be a violation of divine ordinance.

Key MT-LXX divergences: (1) Throughout, the LXX renders qeresh ("frame") as stulos ("pillar"), a significant architectural reinterpretation — pillars and frames produce very different structures. (2) The LXX translates shittim ("acacia") consistently as asepta ("incorruptible wood"), describing the quality rather than the species. (3) In v. 1, the LXX renders khoshev ("skilled craftsman/designer") as huphantos ("weaver"), losing the etymological connection to khashav ("to think, devise") that marks this as the highest textile art. (4) In v. 14, tekhashim is rendered huakinthina ("hyacinth-colored"), interpreting the unknown animal as a color, consistent with the LXX pattern in 25:5. (5) In v. 33, the LXX uses diorieo ("divide, define") for hivdilah, maintaining the separation concept but losing the specific echo of Genesis 1. (6) The LXX uses katapetasma ("curtain") for parokhet — the same term the New Testament uses for the temple veil torn at the crucifixion (Matt 27:51; Mark 15:38; Luke 23:45), making the LXX translation of this chapter the direct terminological bridge between the Exodus tabernacle instructions and the Synoptic passion narratives.
